title: fraxinus biltmoreana var. subcoriacea
intro: Fraxinus biltmoreana var. subcoriacea, also known as Biltmore Ash, is a variety of the Fraxinus biltmoreana tree, native to the southeastern United States. The bark and leaves of this plant have been used in traditional medicine for their medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The plant is believed to have various health benefits, including reducing fever, relieving pain, and treating skin conditions due to its high content of flavonoids and saponins. The herbal preparations of Biltmore Ash include decoctions, infusions, and tinctures, which can be used to treat various health issues, although its use should be consulted with a healthcare professional.
